title: Gendou his desk reflection star of david
display_title: Gendou his desk reflection star of david
wikitext:
[]: Gendou + his desk + reflection = star of david             Quote Post by chiefen » Mon Sep 05, 2005 7:05 am Have anyone else noticed that Gendou and his desk together with the floor's reflection looks like a David star? I haven't seen any comments about it so I just thought I would share. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Mon Sep 05, 2005 8:50 am Never noticed that, at all. But I'd be pretty sure t wasn't intentional.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Sep 05, 2005 3:11 pm How interesting, thanks chiefen. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Magami no D00M » Mon Sep 05, 2005 4:27 pm Yup, just showing that Eva has more to do with Jewish symbols ten Christians'. :P

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Mon Sep 05, 2005 4:44 pm Magami no D00M wrote: Yup, just showing that Eva has more to do with Jewish symbols ten Christians'. :P

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Sep 05, 2005 6:34 pm I wonder? Is this case of coincidence, or a conscious choice on the part of the creators? It's worth noting that Gendou's desk in previous episodes was not this slanted at the sides. This one here from episode #07. [URL=http://imageshack.us] [/URL] The desk was also straight in episode #14. [URL=http://imageshack.us] [/URL] So we are looking at a deliberate redrawing of this silhouette. It was decided for whatever reason not to use the silhouette from episodes #07 and #14. But was it deliberately meant to be a Star of David? Simply Gainix throwing more religious bunting into the show for sleepless otaku to worry over? I can't imagine what Gendou has to do with the Star of David, either in the context of this scene or the show as a whole. Perhaps, if deliberate, this could be taken as proof that all the religious symbolism in Evangelion, really doesn't mean anything at all, besides looking "cool". Edit: P.S. Would this be more at home in the discussion forum?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Mon Sep 05, 2005 7:00 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: But was it deliberately meant to be a Star of David? Simply Gainix throwing more religious bunting into the show for sleepless otaku to worry over?

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Mon Sep 05, 2005 7:46 pm Hm. Ask the guy from the EoE commentary who actually knew which part of the system Sephiroticum that Rei was floating in. I forgot his name. But that guy knew everything. Aoba's voice actor was a dumbass, though. "Hur hur. Is the explosion supposed to be like the one in Hiroshima or Nagasaki?" - Aoba's stupid voice actor Though I wouldn't look too much into the whole slanty desk deal. The animators were underpaid.

[]: ^_ ^ \/,,             Quote Post by chiefen » Mon Sep 05, 2005 7:54 pm Zugzwang wrote: How interesting, thanks chiefen. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Sep 05, 2005 9:12 pm sachiel wrote: Hm. Ask the guy from the EoE commentary who actually knew which part of the system Sephiroticum that Rei was floating in.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Mon Sep 05, 2005 9:52 pm Shin-seiki wrote: sachiel wrote: Hm. Ask the guy from the EoE commentary who actually knew which part of the system Sephiroticum that Rei was floating in.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Wed Sep 07, 2005 2:21 pm I'd noticed it before. It seemed like a reasonable interpretation, considering the "above and below" pattern of Gendo's office. The Magen David is often explained as symbolizing God and Man reaching towards each other...although in EVA, man reaching towards God. Of course, symbols, even ancient ones, are constantly manipulated; this is one of the main ideas of FOUCAULT'S PENDULUM. â€”C.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Sep 07, 2005 2:53 pm Oh man, that book really gave me the sclerosis. It took me almost 1 year to finish it.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Wed Sep 14, 2005 9:28 pm It looked to me like yesod. unless she was in daath which isnt even on the tree as a circle... and it would be upside down.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 15, 2005 4:50 am Strange you should mention it. I noticed it within the last week when I went to #19 to get pictures of Shinji in his cuffs. :P Regardng Yesod... Yeah, Shin-seiki is correct. I figured this out a while ago.

[]:             Quote Post by Oboe » Thu Sep 15, 2005 12:30 pm Whoaaa. How did you manage to get that picture? Did you go into the DVD and stretch out a screenshot?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Sep 15, 2005 1:22 pm Interestingly from the Malkhuth sephira there appears to be two more branchs leading off. I wonder...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 15, 2005 9:49 pm Oboe wrote: Whoaaa. How did you manage to get that picture? Did you go into the DVD and stretch out a screenshot?

[]:             Quote Post by ido » Fri Sep 16, 2005 4:31 am Reichu wrote: Oboe wrote: Whoaaa. How did you manage to get that picture? Did you go into the DVD and stretch out a screenshot?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Sep 28, 2005 2:42 pm I was talking with someone online about that picture and she said this: it looks a bit like the tree of life. but there are a lot more circles. 21 circles. heh. 22 major arcana in the tarot ? the fool is like an extra card - a joker. so that would fit :
